title: Omni-WorldBench: Towards a Comprehensive Interaction-Centric Evaluation for World Models
task: Evaluate interactive response capabilities of world models in 4D settings
key_results: Reveals critical limitations in current world models' ability to reflect action-driven state transitions; evaluates 18 representative models across paradigms
comments: Addresses an important gap by shifting evaluation focus from visual fidelity to causal interaction effects, though practical deployment impact remains unclear.

model_io.model: 18 world models (various paradigms: video generation, 3D reconstruction, 4D)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interactive response is the core capability for 4D world models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; current models show critical limitations in this dimension

title: Speed by Simplicity: A Single-Stream Architecture for Fast Audio-Video Generative Foundation Model
task: Audio-video generation with synchronized speech and human-centric motion
key_results: 5-second 256p video in 2 seconds on H100; 80.0% win rate vs Ovi 1.1; 14.60% WER for speech intelligibility
comments: Single-stream design elegantly simplifies architecture while achieving strong multimodal synchronization; multilingual support is practical asset.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single-stream unified attention can match or exceed multi-stream architectures for synchronized generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves state-of-the-art with simpler design

title: LongCat-Flash-Prover: Advancing Native Formal Reasoning via Agentic Tool-Integrated Reinforcement Learning
task: Native formal reasoning in Lean4 via auto-formalization, sketching, and proving
key_results: 97.1% pass rate on MiniF2F-Test with 72 inference budget; 70.8% on ProverBench; 41.5% on PutnamBench
comments: MoE architecture with hierarchical importance sampling provides stable training for long-horizon formal reasoning; notable improvement over open-weights baselines.

inputs: informal problem statement or formal claim

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decomposing formal reasoning into auto-formalization, sketching, and proving enables more stable learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA with sample-efficient training

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical importance sampling stabilizes MoE training on long-horizon tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HisPO algorithm prevents reward hacking and model divergence

title: VideoDetective: Clue Hunting via both Extrinsic Query and Intrinsic Relevance for Long Video Understanding
task: Localize query-relevant segments in long videos for question answering
key_results: Up to 7.5% accuracy improvement on VideoMME-long across multiple MLLMs
comments: Hypothesis-Verification-Refinement loop with visual-temporal affinity graphs is intuitive; approach is agnostic to MLLM choice.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Propagating relevance scores via inter-segment affinity improves long-video understanding over query-only localization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent gains across benchmarks

title: Look Where It Matters: High-Resolution Crops Retrieval for Efficient VLMs
task: Resolve accuracy-efficiency trade-off in VLMs by retrieving task-relevant high-resolution crops
key_results: Maintains accuracy while reducing computational cost through selective high-resolution processing
comments: Tool-calling mechanism for crop retrieval is practical; automatic supervised data construction enables scalable training.

model_io.model: AwaRes (VLM with tool-calling for crop retrieval)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Selective high-resolution processing via tool-calling preserves accuracy while improving efficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves accuracy-efficiency trade-off improvements

title: SpatialBoost: Enhancing Visual Representation through Language-Guided Reasoning
task: Enhance spatial awareness of vision encoders via language-guided reasoning
key_results: 3.8% mIoU improvement on ADE20K (55.9 to 59.7) for DINOv3
comments: LLM-based linguistic injection of 3D spatial knowledge is creative; multi-turn CoT reasoning progressively builds hierarchical understanding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Linguistic description of 3D spatial structure can improve vision encoder embeddings for spatial tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA on multiple 3D perception benchmarks

title: F4Splat: Feed-Forward Predictive Densification for Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting
task: Adaptive Gaussian allocation for feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting
key_results: Superior novel-view synthesis vs prior uncalibrated feed-forward methods with significantly fewer Gaussians
comments: Densification-score-guided allocation elegantly addresses redundancy; explicit Gaussian budget control without retraining is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatially adaptive Gaussian allocation reduces redundancy in simple regions and across overlapping views
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves quality gains with fewer Gaussians

title: OpenResearcher: A Fully Open Pipeline for Long-Horizon Deep Research Trajectory Synthesis
task: Synthesize long-horizon research trajectories for agent training via offline search-and-browse
key_results: 54.8% on BrowseComp-Plus (+34.0 vs base); 97K trajectories synthesized including 100+ tool-call sequences
comments: Fully offline, reproducible pipeline decouples corpus from trajectory synthesis; practical insights into deep research agent design are valuable.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Offline trajectory synthesis with explicit browser primitives enables stable, reproducible agent training
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves competitive performance without proprietary APIs

title: Manifold-Aware Exploration for Reinforcement Learning in Video Generation
task: Stabilize GRPO alignment for video generation through manifold-aware exploration
key_results: Consistent gains on HunyuanVideo1.5 across VQ, MQ, TA metrics; superior video quality vs prior GRPO methods
comments: Manifold-aware SDE with logarithmic curvature correction is principled; dual trust region prevents long-horizon drift effectively.

outputs: video samples with improved alignment to rewards

hypotheses.hypothesis: Constraining exploration to valid video data manifold stabilizes GRPO for video generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves better reliability than unconstrained GRPO

title: mSFT: Addressing Dataset Mixtures Overfiting Heterogeneously in Multi-task SFT
task: Optimize multi-task data mixtures to prevent heterogeneous overfitting in SFT
key_results: Consistent improvements across 10 benchmarks and 6 models; can improve performance while lowering FLOPs at low compute budgets
comments: Overfitting-aware search identifies and excludes early-overfitting tasks; simple but effective approach to mixture optimization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Heterogeneous task learning dynamics cause differential overfitting that can be mitigated through selective data exclusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mSFT outperforms uniform mixture strategies

title: Group3D: MLLM-Driven Semantic Grouping for Open-Vocabulary 3D Object Detection
task: Open-vocabulary 3D object detection via semantic grouping from MLLMs
key_results: SOTA on ScanNet and ARKitScenes; strong generalization in zero-shot scenarios
comments: Integrating semantic constraints during instance merging (not post-hoc) is elegant; taxonomy-aware vocabulary groups prevent over-merging.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic compatibility groups derived from MLLMs can improve geometry-only instance merging in open-vocab detection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduces over-merging and improves label assignment

title: RoboAlign: Learning Test-Time Reasoning for Language-Action Alignment in Vision-Language-Action Models
task: Improve vision-language-action model performance via test-time reasoning alignment
key_results: 17.5% on LIBERO, 18.9% on CALVIN, 106.6% on real-world tasks vs SFT; achieved with <1% additional data
comments: Bridging modality gap between language and low-level actions via RL is well-motivated; dual-agent architecture (reasoning + summary) adds robustness.

model_io.model: RoboAlign (MLLM + diffusion action head + RL alignment)

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL-based alignment refines zero-shot language-to-action reasoning to improve VLA performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; substantial gains on diverse robotics benchmarks

title: Repurposing Geometric Foundation Models for Multi-view Diffusion
task: Multi-view diffusion via geometrically consistent latent space from foundation models
key_results: Outperforms VAE/RAE latent spaces on 2D/3D metrics; 4.4x training acceleration vs VAE
comments: Repurposing geometric foundation model features as latent space is clever; validates geometric consistency aids NVS generation.

model_io.model: Geometric Latent Diffusion (GLD diffusion in geometric feature space)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometrically consistent latent spaces from foundation models improve multi-view diffusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves competitive results without large-scale text-to-image pretraining

title: BubbleRAG: Evidence-Driven Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Black-Box Knowledge Graphs
task: Retrieve evidence subgraphs from black-box knowledge graphs for RAG via optimal informative subgraph retrieval
key_results: SOTA on multi-hop QA benchmarks; improved F1 and accuracy while remaining plug-and-play
comments: Formulating retrieval as NP-hard Optimal Informative Subgraph Retrieval (OISR) is principled; training-free pipeline is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic anchor grouping and bubble expansion improve both recall and precision over black-box KGs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA on multi-hop benchmarks

title: On the Direction of RLVR Updates for LLM Reasoning: Identification and Exploitation
task: Analyze and exploit the direction of RLVR updates for improved reasoning
key_results: Δlog p more effectively identifies reasoning-critical updates than magnitude metrics; test-time extrapolation improves accuracy without retraining
comments: Signed token-level log probability difference provides cleaner signal than magnitude; practical applications at test/train time.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Direction of RLVR updates (signed Δlog p) is more informative than magnitude for understanding reasoning improvements
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables test-time extrapolation and training-time reweighting gains

title: SEM: Sparse Embedding Modulation for Post-Hoc Debiasing of Vision-Language Models
task: Post-hoc debiasing of vision-language models via sparse latent representation
key_results: Substantial fairness gains across benchmarks; preserves semantic fidelity while removing bias
comments: Operating in SAE latent space provides better disentanglement than dense CLIP embeddings; enables precise non-linear bias removal.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sparse latent representations provide better bias-semantics disentanglement than dense embeddings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves fairness gains without degrading utility

title: Insight-V++: Towards Advanced Long-Chain Visual Reasoning with Multimodal Large Language Models
task: Generate long-chain visual reasoning trajectories and train MLLMs with dual-agent architecture and RL refinement
key_results: Significant gains on image/video reasoning benchmarks; ST-GRPO and J-GRPO improve spatial-temporal reasoning
comments: Multi-granularity assessment for autonomous trajectory synthesis is scalable; dual-agent design (reasoning + summary) improves trajectory quality.

model_io.model: Insight-V++ (dual-agent reasoning + summary MLLM with ST-GRPO/J-GRPO)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-agent visual reasoning with RL refinement improves spatial-temporal understanding over supervised learning alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ST-GRPO/J-GRPO enable substantial gains on complex reasoning tasks

title: Scaling DoRA: High-Rank Adaptation via Factored Norms and Fused Kernels
task: Accelerate high-rank DoRA via factored norm computation and fused Triton kernels
key_results: 1.5-2.0x inference speedup, 1.5-1.9x faster gradient computation; up to 7 GB lower peak VRAM vs Hugging Face PEFT
comments: Factored norm eliminates memory-intensive dense product; fused kernels reduce traffic and numerical instability effectively.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Factored norm decomposition enables practical high-rank DoRA on single-GPU setups
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves major speedups with stable numerical precision

title: PivotRL: High Accuracy Agentic Post-Training at Low Compute Cost
task: Post-training agentic tasks via on-policy RL on existing SFT trajectories at low compute cost
key_results: +4.17% in-domain, +10.04% OOD accuracy over SFT; competitive with E2E RL using 4x fewer rollout turns
comments: Pivot identification via high-variance actions and functional-equivalent rewards efficiently capture RL benefits; practical for production scaling.

model_io.model: PivotRL (local on-policy RL with pivot filtering)
outputs: refined policy with improved OOD generalization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Local on-policy RL on high-variance intermediate turns preserves OOD while improving in-domain accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PivotRL achieves E2E RL benefits at SFT-like cost

title: Generalized Discrete Diffusion from Snapshots
task: Discrete diffusion modeling for large-vocabulary text generation via snapshot-based ELBO
key_results: Outperforms existing discrete diffusion and beats autoregressive models at scale for first time
comments: Snapshot-based ELBO enables efficient training without full noising path; generalization beyond discrete domains promising.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Snapshot-based ELBO enables efficient discrete diffusion training for large vocabularies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms prior discrete and autoregressive baselines

title: REVERE: Reflective Evolving Research Engineer for Scientific Workflows
task: Continuously evolve research coding agent behavior via reflection on global failure patterns
key_results: 4.50% improvement on SUPER, 3.51% on ResearchCodeBench, 4.89% on ScienceAgentBench over expert-crafted instructions
comments: Global training context and reflection-based prompt evolution avoid local overfitting; reusable heuristics distill recurring patterns effectively.

model_io.model: REVERE (reflective agent with multi-field prompt evolution)
outputs: evolved system prompt, task template, and cheatsheet

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reflecting on global training context and distilling heuristics improves research agent generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms fixed expert-crafted instructions

title: Agentic AI and the next intelligence explosion
task: Conceptual analysis of agentic AI as plural, social reasoning systems
key_results: No empirical results; position paper on institutional alignment and societies of thought
comments: Conceptual framing of AI singularity as plural societies rather than monolithic mind is novel; suggests institutional design as scaling path.

title: The Universal Normal Embedding
task: Align generative and encoding latent spaces via shared Gaussian geometry
key_results: Linear probes in both DDIM and encoder spaces achieve aligned attribute predictions; enables faithful, controllable edits
comments: Universal Normal Embedding hypothesis provides unifying view of generative/encoder latent spaces; empirical support on CelebA is suggestive but limited.

outputs: per-image DDIM noise and encoder embeddings for alignment analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Generative and encoding latent spaces share underlying Gaussian-like geometry with aligned linear directions
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; linear probes align, but broader applicability unclear

title: Effective Strategies for Asynchronous Software Engineering Agents
task: Coordinate asynchronous multi-agent collaboration for long-horizon software engineering via centralized delegation
key_results: 26.7% improvement on PaperBench, 14.3% on Commit0 over single-agent; branch-and-merge is critical coordination mechanism
comments: CAID framework elegantly applies git primitives (worktree, commit, merge) to multi-agent coordination; isolated workspaces prevent interference.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structured asynchronous coordination with isolated workspaces and test-based verification improves multi-agent SWE performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves significant accuracy gains

title: WorldCache: Content-Aware Caching for Accelerated Video World Models
task: Accelerate video world model inference via content-aware feature caching
key_results: 2.3x inference speedup on Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B while preserving 99.4% quality; outperforms prior training-free caching
comments: Motion-adaptive thresholds and saliency-weighted drift estimation address limitations of Zero-Order Hold assumption; phase-aware scheduling is principled.

model_io.model: WorldCache (motion-adaptive caching for DiT-based video models)
outputs: reused/blended/warped features for next denoising step

hypotheses.hypothesis: Motion-adaptive, saliency-weighted caching with blending/warping improves feature reuse quality in video diffusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves major speedup with minimal quality loss

title: AnimalCLAP: Taxonomy-Aware Language-Audio Pretraining for Species Recognition and Trait Inference
task: Recognize animal species and infer ecological traits from vocalizations via taxonomy-aware language-audio pretraining
key_results: AnimalCLAP dataset of 4,225 hours (6,823 species) with 22 ecological traits; outperforms CLAP on species recognition
comments: Taxonomy-aware design leverages hierarchical biological structure; dataset and model will enable future ecosystem monitoring research.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Taxonomy-aware alignment improves zero-shot species recognition from unseen vocalizations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior generalization vs non-taxonomy CLAP

title: Perceptio: Perception Enhanced Vision Language Models via Spatial Token Generation
task: Enhance VLM spatial grounding via explicit semantic segmentation and depth token generation
key_results: +0.8/+1.4/+1.1 cIoU on RefCOCO/+/g; +10.3% HardBLINK; +1.0% MMBench
comments: Emitting spatial tokens before answering is intuitive chain-of-thought approach; composite depth-token objectives and soft-merging ensure stable training.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit spatial chain-of-thought via segmentation/depth tokens improves fine-grained spatial grounding in LVLMs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA across spatial reasoning benchmarks

title: MemDLM: Memory-Enhanced DLM Training
task: Train Diffusion Language Models with reduced train-inference mismatch via Parametric Memory from bi-level optimization
key_results: Faster convergence, lower training loss; emergent in-weight retrieval helps Needle-in-a-Haystack long-context tasks
comments: Embedding simulated denoising into training via bi-level optimization is principled; Parametric Memory acts as novel emergent mechanism.

model_io.model: MemDLM (Diffusion LM with bi-level memory optimization)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parametric Memory from bi-level optimization reduces train-inference mismatch in DLMs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables better convergence and long-context understanding

title: Demystifying Reinforcement Learning for Long-Horizon Tool-Using Agents: A Comprehensive Recipe
task: Systematically decompose agentic RL design space for long-horizon tool-using tasks
key_results: SOTA on TravelPlanner; reward/algorithm choices scale-dependent; ~1K balanced samples optimal; environmental stability critical
comments: Controlled empirical study provides practical recipe; key insights (scale-dependent reward shaping, sample efficiency) valuable for practitioners.

model_io.model: LLM agent with RL training on TravelPlanner environment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reward shaping, model scale, data composition, and algorithm choice interact in scale-dependent ways for agentic RL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; systematic study reveals optimal trade-offs

title: Not All Layers Are Created Equal: Adaptive LoRA Ranks for Personalized Image Generation
task: Adaptive LoRA ranks for personalized image generation via importance-ordered variational selection
key_results: Competitive visual quality across subjects with significantly lower memory and rank than fixed high-rank LoRA
comments: Encouraging adaptive ranks during fine-tuning avoids combinatorial search; importance ordering effectively prevents unnecessary rank growth.

model_io.model: LoRA² (diffusion model with adaptive per-layer ranks)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Allowing adaptive per-layer LoRA ranks during fine-tuning improves efficiency without quality loss
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves competitive quality with lower memory than fixed high-rank

title: SNAP: Speaker Nulling for Artifact Projection in Speech Deepfake Detection
task: Detect speech deepfakes via speaker subspace nulling to isolate synthesis artifacts
key_results: SOTA deepfake detection; reduces speaker entanglement to enable detection of unseen speakers
comments: Identifying speaker entanglement as key limitation is valuable; orthogonal projection cleanly separates speaker from synthesis artifact information.

model_io.model: SNAP (speaker subspace nulling via orthogonal projection)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Suppressing speaker-dependent components enables detectors to learn synthesis-artifact cues for better generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA on cross-speaker evaluation

title: Safe Flow Q-Learning: Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning with Reachability-Based Flow Policies
task: Offline safe RL via reachability-inspired safety value functions and one-step flow policies
key_results: Matches/exceeds prior safe RL performance with substantially lower inference latency; conformal calibration ensures safety coverage
comments: Combining Hamilton-Jacobi reachability with efficient one-step flow policies is elegant; important for real-time safety-critical deployment.

model_io.model: SafeFQL (safety value function + one-step flow policy)

hypotheses.hypothesis: One-step flow policies with reachability-based safety values enable efficient safe offline RL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves fast inference with safety guarantees

title: AdditiveLLM2: A Multi-modal Large Language Model for Additive Manufacturing
task: Domain-adapted LLM for additive manufacturing via instruction tuning on journal articles
key_results: >90% accuracy on additive manufacturing domain tasks; proficiency on language and vision tasks
comments: Accessible specialization method using 50M tokens from open-access data; demonstrates practical domain adaptation for technical domains.

model_io.model: AdditiveLLM2 (Gemma 3 instruction tuned on AM data)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Instruction tuning on domain-specific journal articles enables effective domain adaptation for technical fields
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves >90% on domain-specific benchmarks

title: FluidWorld: Reaction-Diffusion Dynamics as a Predictive Substrate for World Models
task: Video prediction via reaction-diffusion PDEs as alternative to Transformer-based predictors
key_results: 2x lower reconstruction error, 10-15% higher spatial structure preservation vs Transformer/ConvLSTM; maintains coherent multi-step rollouts
comments: PDE-based approach provides O(N) spatial complexity and adaptive computation vs O(N²) attention; interesting proof-of-concept for alternative architectures.

outputs: predicted future frames via PDE integration

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reaction-diffusion PDEs can match or exceed Transformer attention for video prediction with better multi-step stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms baselines on rollout coherence despite comparable parameters

title: Understanding Behavior Cloning with Action Quantization
task: Analyze quantization error propagation in behavior cloning with autoregressive models
key_results: Optimal sample complexity with log-loss; polynomial horizon dependence on quantization error under stability/smoothness conditions
comments: Theoretical analysis of widespread quantization practice fills important gap; proposes model-based augmentation without policy smoothness requirement.

model_io.model: Behavior cloning with action quantization (theoretical analysis)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Behavior cloning with quantized actions achieves optimal sample complexity under dynamical stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; theoretical characterization matches empirical practice

title: Semantic Audio-Visual Navigation in Continuous Environments
task: Audio-visual navigation in continuous 3D environments with memory-augmented goal reasoning
key_results: 12.1% absolute improvement in success rate over SOTA; robust to short-duration sounds and long-distance navigation
comments: MAGNet design that jointly encodes spatial and semantic goals is well-motivated; memory-augmented reasoning handles silences effectively.

model_io.model: MAGNet (multimodal transformer with memory for audio-visual navigation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Memory-augmented goal reasoning improves audio-visual navigation robustness to sound intermittency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA with strong generalization

title: In-the-Wild Camouflage Attack on Vehicle Detectors through Controllable Image Editing
task: Generate adversarial camouflage for vehicles via conditional image editing with structural fidelity
key_results: >38% AP50 decrease on COCO/LINZ; better human-perceived stealthiness than baselines; transfers to black-box detectors
comments: Formulating camouflage as image-editing with unified objective (structure, style, adversarial) is effective; practical concerns about misuse.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conditional image editing can generate semantically meaningful, transferable adversarial camouflage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves strong attack effectiveness with human stealthiness

title: Progressive Training for Explainable Citation-Grounded Dialogue: Reducing Hallucination to Zero in English-Hindi LLMs
task: Train explainable knowledge-grounded dialogue in bilingual setting with citation grounding and post-hoc analysis
key_results: Zero hallucination in encoder-decoder models from Stage 2; 0.0% hallucination rate with 14.60% WER for speech; prevents forgetting
comments: Progressive four-stage pipeline and systematic post-hoc explainability analysis (attention, Integrated Gradients, occlusion) provide transparency.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Citation-grounded SFT from Stage 2 onward eliminates hallucination in encoder-decoder models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 0.0% hallucination maintained through GRPO

title: ToolRosetta: Bridging Open-Source Repositories and Large Language Model Agents through Automated Tool Standardization
task: Automatically standardize heterogeneous open-source code as MCP-compatible tools for LLM agents
key_results: Successfully automates tool standardization; improves task completion over commercial LLMs via tool integration
comments: Unified framework for diverse codebases via MCP; security inspection layer mitigates risks effectively.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Automated repository-to-tool translation enables reliable LLM agent access to diverse open-source code
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improves task completion over baseline LLMs

title: Structure and function of mouse lens suture examined by 2-photon fluorescence microscopic imaging
task: Characterize lens suture architecture and permeability in normal vs. KLPH-knockout mice
key_results: KLPH-KO lenses showed significantly increased suture pattern randomization (p < 0.05) with mean SSIM 2-5 μm central voids containing amorphous structures
WT lenses displayed typical Y and double-Y anterior sutures; KLPH-KO exhibited heterogeneous morphologies and anterior-posterior misalignment
comments: The use of 2-photon microscopy enables in vivo 3D imaging without requiring tissue sections, providing unprecedented morphological detail of suture defects and cataract formation mechanisms.

model_io.model: Mouse lens fiber and suture system (wild-type and KLPH-KO)

hypotheses.hypothesis: KLPH protein stabilizes lens fiber organization and suture morphology
hypotheses.result: KLPH-KO lenses exhibit significantly heterogeneous sutures with increased pattern randomization and enlarged central voids

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lens sutures function as permeability barriers and ion/nutrient transport pathways
hypotheses.result: Dye-impermeable suture interface demonstrated; KLPH loss disrupts this barrier function as shown by altered morphology and subcellular aggregate accumulation

title: Non-classic deubiquitinase USP13 inhibits bladder cancer metastasis through destabilizing cytoplasmic KDM3A
task: Clarify USP13-mediated KDM3A destabilization in bladder cancer metastasis suppression
key_results: USP13 deubiquitinates KDM3A K63-linked chains, promoting K48-ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation; USP13 is downregulated in bladder cancer; USP13-mediated KDM3A destabilization inhibits metastasis in vivo
comments: Non-classic K63-to-K48 ubiquitin relay mechanism; Note: study was retracted for contaminated cell line and resubmitted.

model_io.model: Bladder cancer cell lines and bladder-injected liver metastasis xenografts

hypotheses.hypothesis: USP13 suppresses bladder cancer metastasis by destabilizing cytoplasmic KDM3A
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in vivo; USP13 downregulation associates with poor prognosis

title: The ATC12 small molecule inhibits the Aurora-A/TPX2 interaction and impairs the proliferation of breast cancer cells
task: Evaluate ATC12 small molecule Aurora-A/TPX2 interaction inhibitor in breast cancer
key_results: ATC12 binds Aurora-A and competes with TPX2; impairs Aurora-A/TPX2 complex formation; decreases viability and proliferation in 2D/3D cultures and patient organoids
comments: Protein-protein interaction inhibitors targeting non-catalytic oncogenic functions offer advantages over ATP-competitive inhibitors that show limited clinical efficacy.

model_io.model: Breast cancer cell lines, mammospheres, patient-derived organoids

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aurora-A/TPX2 protein-protein interaction is targetable and essential for cancer cell proliferation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ATC12-mediated inhibition reduces viability across model systems

title: Dynamic epigenetic regulation of BCLAF1 splicing in acute myeloid leukemia
task: Determine epigenetic regulation of BCLAF1 splicing in acute myeloid leukemia
key_results: Full-length BCLAF1 is oncogenic; short-length isoform is tumor suppressive; HDAC1-DNMT3A interplay controls isoform balance; epidrugs restore equilibrium
comments: This splicing dysregulation mechanism represents a druggable vulnerability in AML with potential for re-sensitizing treatment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: HDAC1-DNMT3A axis regulates BCLAF1 isoform balance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; epidrugs restore physiological equilibrium

title: Arginine dependency in omental metastasis of epithelial ovarian cancer reveals a therapeutic vulnerability
task: Identify arginine dependency in ovarian cancer omental metastasis
key_results: EOC omental metastasis depends on exogenous arginine; arginine binds DDX3X promoting nuclear retention and DDR gene transcription via ATM/CHK2/P53; dietary arginine deprivation suppresses metastasis
comments: The metabolic-epigenetic coupling through arginine-DDX3X-DDR represents a non-canonical amino acid vulnerability distinct from proliferative dependency.

model_io.model: EOC cell lines and omental metastasis mouse models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Arginine-DDX3X interaction enables DDR activation under metastatic stress
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; arginine restriction and DDX3X inhibition suppress metastasis

title: Malic enzyme 2 suppresses PINK1-Parkin-mediated mitophagy by stabilizing ATAD3A via competitive interaction with TRIM25
task: Define ME2's role in mitophagy suppression via ATAD3A stabilization
key_results: ME2 depletion enhances PINK1-Parkin mitophagy; ME2 competes with E3 ligase TRIM25 for ATAD3A binding; ATAD3A ubiquitination and degradation increase PINK1 levels; hyperactivated mitophagy suppresses hepatoma proliferation
comments: This ME2-ATAD3A-PINK1 axis links metabolic enzyme function to mitochondrial quality control; metabolic-autophagy crosstalk is emerging as a cancer vulnerability.

model_io.model: Hepatoma cell lines with ME2 knockdown/pharmacological mitophagy inhibition

hypotheses.hypothesis: ME2 suppresses mitophagy by stabilizing ATAD3A through competitive TRIM25 inhibition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ME2 knockdown enhances mitophagy and reduces proliferation

title: Redox cycling nitroxide limits cellular iron availability and selectively inhibits iron-sulfur cluster metabolism
task: Determine Tempol's iron-limiting mechanism and effects on iron-sulfur cluster metabolism
key_results: Tempol limits iron bioavailability via ascorbate-iron redox balance, triggering IRP2/HIF1α upregulation and partial ISC synthesis suppression; inhibits ferroptosis
comments: The distinction from iron chelators and reliance on redox cycling rather than direct chelation is mechanistically important for understanding iron homeostasis.

model_io.model: Multiple cell lines with varying ISC protein dependencies

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ascorbate/iron redox balance is essential for ISC homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ascorbate reverses Tempol effects on ISC proteins

title: ATP6V1B2 alleviates hepatic steatosis by promoting lysosomal acidification in hepatocytes
task: Establish ATP6V1B2's role in hepatic lipid metabolism via lysosomal acidification
key_results: ATP6V1B2 downregulation increases lipid accumulation and FASN upregulation; ATP6V1B2 promotes lysosomal FASN degradation; ATP6V1B2 is reduced in MASLD patient serum
comments: V-ATPase subunit mutations present a tractable metabolic target for fatty liver disease without directly targeting lipogenic enzymes.

model_io.model: Hepatocyte cell lines and MASLD patient samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: ATP6V1B2 maintains lysosomal acidification required for FASN degradation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ATP6V1B2 loss impairs FASN lysosomal catabolism

title: Bilirubin directly activates RIPK3 to induce non-classical necroptosis
task: Characterize bilirubin-induced non-canonical RIPK3-mediated necroptosis
key_results: Bilirubin directly binds RIPK3 residues D161/S102, triggering MLKL activation without RIPK1 or RHIM domains; Ripk3 depletion reduces bilirubin neurotoxicity in mice
comments: This non-canonical activation pathway bypasses traditional death receptor signaling, revealing a metabolic trigger for necroptosis.

model_io.model: Cell lines and murine neurodegeneration models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bilirubin-RIPK3 interaction is RIPK1 and RHIM-independent
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; necroptosis occurs without canonical pathway components

title: Cardiomyocyte Cyclin-dependent kinase 9 directly binds to and phosphorylates NF-κB p65 subunit to drive cardiac inflammation and remodeling
task: Identify CDK9 as a cardiac inflammation driver via NF-κB phosphorylation
key_results: CDK9 T186A mutation attenuates Ang II-induced heart remodeling; CDK9 directly phosphorylates NF-κB p65 independent of IKKβ and RNAPII pathways
comments: Cell cycle-independent CDK9 function in terminally differentiated cardiomyocytes is noteworthy; therapeutic inhibition was validated in mouse models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CDK9 Thr-186 phosphorylation is required for direct p65 binding and activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; T186A blocks inflammation, T186E enhances it

title: Transient molecular chimerism for exploiting xenogeneic organelles
task: Characterize transient molecular chimerism in kleptoplast photosynthesis
key_results: Nuclear-encoded RvRbcS-like and RuBisCO activase support photosynthesis in acquired Tetraselmis kleptoplasts; RvRbcS-like has carboxyl-terminal extension for pyrenoid reorganization; knockdown/knockout impair photosynthesis
comments: This represents a naturally occurring transient xenogeneic organelle system distinct from constitutive chimerism; de novo transport system assembly after acquisition is mechanistically unique.

model_io.model: Rapaza viridis flagellate with kleptoplast acquisition from Tetraselmis

hypotheses.hypothesis: RvRbcS-like protein supports both photosynthesis and kleptoplast remodeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; knockdown/knockout impair both processes

title: Tuft cells shape airway remodeling by eliciting OXGR1- and SOX9-dependent stem cell programs
task: Elucidate tuft cell signaling in airway remodeling via cysteinyl leukotriene-OXGR1-SOX9 axis
key_results: Tuft cells generate CysLTs activating OXGR1 on SOX9+ gland progenitors; SOX9 deletion prevents remodeling and restores epithelial architecture
comments: This tuft cell-immune circuit represents a critical regulatory node; the finding has translational relevance for human sinus mucosa pathology.

model_io.model: Murine airway injury models with fate mapping and null strains

hypotheses.hypothesis: SOX9 is required for aberrant repair and remodeling downstream of OXGR1
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOX9 deletion prevents remodeling despite OXGR1 signaling

title: Hh and EGFR-Ras signaling promote distinct steps of tumor progression in the Drosophila follicle epithelium
task: Dissect Hedgehog and EGFR-Ras pathway roles in Drosophila follicle stem cell tumorigenesis
key_results: Hedgehog activates EMT transcription factor Zfh1 and undifferentiated state; EGFR-Ras induces cell cycle defects via Pointed/E2f1; combined overactivation blocks differentiation and induces tumor-like growth
comments: Single-cell transcriptomics reveals transcriptional convergence; pathway cooperation occurs at differentiation checkpoints rather than proliferation alone.

model_io.model: Drosophila follicle epithelium with pathway overactivation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hedgehog and EGFR-Ras converge transcriptionally to prevent differentiation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; combined activation induces tumor-like phenotype with sustained proliferation

title: Mitochondrial metabolic imbalance drives diploidization in mouse haploid embryonic stem cells via NADPH overload
task: Identify mitochondrial metabolic imbalance as root cause of haploid genome diploidization
key_results: Haploid ESCs show reduced respiration, altered redox balance, and NADPH overload; NADPH oxidation stabilizes haploidy; reduced AURORA kinase activation impairs mitotic regulation
comments: This metabolic-karyotypic link reveals how redox homeostasis couples to chromosome stability; counterselection of haploidy appears driven by metabolic constraints.

model_io.model: Mouse haploid embryonic stem cells with metabolic profiling

hypotheses.hypothesis: NADPH overload from reduced respiration triggers diploidization via impaired AURORA activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NADPH oxidation stabilizes haploid state

title: Nonsense-mediated mRNA decay safeguards telomeres in pluripotent stem cells
task: Establish nonsense-mediated mRNA decay's role in telomere protection via Trf1 quality control
key_results: NMD is essential for survival when TRF2 is absent; NMD degrades aberrant Trf1 mRNA preventing truncated TRF1 production; truncated TRF1 impairs telomere protection
comments: This reveals an unexpected RNA quality control mechanism safeguarding genome stability in pluripotent cells; RNA-mediated telomere protection is unusual.

model_io.model: Mouse embryonic stem cells with TRF2 deletion and NMD modulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: NMD-mediated Trf1 mRNA quality control compensates for TRF2 loss in pluripotent cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NMD depletion causes telomere damage when TRF2 is absent

title: Hypoxia and aspirin additively increase intracellular glutamine accumulation in PIK3CA-mutated colorectal cancer cells
task: Determine additive effects of hypoxia and aspirin on glutamine accumulation in PIK3CA-mutant colorectal cancer
key_results: Aspirin and hypoxia additively increase intracellular glutamine in PIK3CA-MT cells via enhanced ASCT2 amino acid uptake; V-9302 (ASCT2 inhibitor) with aspirin shows antitumor potential
comments: Metabolic-microenvironmental interaction reveals tumor-intrinsic vulnerability; glutamine transporter targeting synergizes with aspirin therapy.

model_io.model: PIK3CA-WT/MT isogenic SW48 cells and HCT116 cells with hypoxia and aspirin

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hypoxia and aspirin synergistically increase glutamine via ASCT2-dependent uptake in PIK3CA-MT cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; V-9302 reverses accumulation and enhances aspirin efficacy

title: Ambrisentan attenuates cisplatin-related mitochondrial dysfunction in the heart via regulation of p53 and NF-κB signaling
task: Evaluate ambrisentan protection against cisplatin-induced cardiotoxicity via p53/NF-κB regulation
key_results: Ambrisentan reduces cisplatin-induced apoptosis (↓caspase-3/7, ↑Bcl-2), inflammation (↓TNFα/IL6), and mitochondrial damage (↓ROS, promotes fusion, enhances biogenesis); restores mitochondrial function
comments: ETA antagonism provides pleiotropic cardioprotection; mechanistic convergence on p53/NF-κB suggests pathway crosstalk in chemotoxicity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: p53 and NF-κB inhibition enhance ambrisentan's protective effects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pharmacological pathway inhibition augments cardioprotection

title: Interplay between DYRK1A dosage and aneuploidy-induced neuropathology in Down syndrome
task: Clarify DYRK1A dosage and aneuploidy interactions in Down syndrome neuropathology
key_results: Trisomy 21 aneuploidy induces protein aggregates and neuronal apoptosis; DYRK1A overdosage activates NLRP3 inflammasome in astrocytes; DYRK1A copy number correction plus chemical chaperone rescues apoptosis
comments: The dual aneuploidy-gene dosage mechanism highlights how therapeutic strategies must address both global cellular stress and specific dosage imbalances.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DYRK1A overdosage and aneuploidy-induced stress synergistically enhance apoptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; DYRK1A correction plus chaperone rescues neuronal death

title: Genetic diagnosis of sibling cases initiated by identification of outlier gene expression using transcriptome analysis of urine-derived cells
task: Genetic diagnosis of muscular dystrophy-dystroglycanopathy via RNA-seq outlier detection
key_results: RNA-seq of urine-derived cells detected markedly decreased B3GALNT2 expression (OUTRIDER); genome sequencing identified paternally inherited 357-bp promoter deletion; segregated with phenotype in siblings
comments: Proof-of-concept that transcriptome-guided outlier analysis can identify non-coding second hits; demonstrates practical diagnostic utility of RNA-seq in genetic disease.

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNA-seq-guided analysis identifies non-coding second pathogenic hits in compound heterozygous variants
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; promoter deletion identified and functionally validated by RNA-seq

title: Post-pandemic changes in population immunity have reduced the likelihood of emergence of zoonotic coronaviruses
task: Impact of post-pandemic population immunity on zoonotic coronavirus emergence risk
key_results: Cross-neutralizing antibodies against spike proteins of multiple zoonotic sarbecoviruses present in post-COVID sera; mathematical modeling predicts emergence likelihood of novel sarbecovirus significantly reduced by population cross-immunity; extent depends on cross-protection level and R₀
comments: Timely analysis of pandemic-generated immunity as barrier to spillover; highlights vaccine strategy implications for future pandemic prevention.

inputs: Sera from individuals with different COVID-19 immunological histories; sarbecovirus S protein sequences
outputs: Cross-neutralizing antibody titers; emergence probability predictions

hypotheses.hypothesis: SARS-CoV-2 circulation and vaccination generate cross-protection against zoonotic sarbecoviruses, reducing emergence likelihood
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; population immunity creates immunological barrier to novel sarbecovirus emergence

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-specificity SARS-CoV-2 vaccine could increase emergence risk by suppressing SARS-CoV-2 prevalence and natural cross-protection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in simulations; vaccine specificity-emergence tradeoff identified

title: AI-based chest X-ray prioritization in the lung cancer diagnostic pathway: the LungIMPACT randomized controlled trial
task: AI prioritization of chest X-rays in lung cancer diagnostic pathway (RCT)
key_results: No significant difference in median time to CT with/without AI prioritization (53 vs 53 days, ratio of geometric means 0.97, 95% CI 0.93–1.02, P = 0.31); median time to diagnosis 44 vs 46 days (ratio 0.98, P = 0.84); 30.3% discordance between AI and radiologist
comments: Null RCT result demonstrates that AI prioritization alone does not accelerate lung cancer diagnosis in UK primary care; questions deployment strategy without systemic workflow integration.

outputs: Prioritization flag; AI report; concordance with radiologist

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI prioritization of CXR findings accelerates time to CT and lung cancer diagnosis
hypotheses.result: Rejected; no significant time difference (geometric mean ratio 0.97, P = 0.31)

title: Clustering the protein universe of life using DIAMOND DeepClust
task: Planetary-scale protein clustering across the tree of life
key_results: 19 billion biosphere proteins aggregated into 544 million nonsingleton clusters; DeepClust database improves AlphaFold2 structure prediction
comments: Impressive scale and computational efficiency, but specific improvement metrics for structure prediction are not quantified.

outputs: 544 million protein clusters; cluster database for structure prediction enhancement

hypotheses.hypothesis: Clustered protein information from DeepClust can enhance AlphaFold2 predictions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; structure prediction improvements shown

title: 2D Multimodal Image Collection for Fluorescence Prediction from Transmitted Light Microscopy
task: Fluorescence prediction from transmitted light microscopy images
key_results: Dataset of 56,984 standardized 2D images from 2,574 acquisition sets across 8 imaging centers; supports training generalizable deep learning models for in silico labeling and segmentation
comments: Large-scale, well-curated dataset with rich metadata should enable robust model development, though actual ML model performance metrics are not provided.

inputs: Transmitted light images (bright-field, phase contrast, DIC)
outputs: Predicted fluorescence images (nucleus, mitochondria, tubulin, actin)

title: Design, synthesis, molecular docking and cytotoxic evaluation of novel pyrimidine-based sulfonamide derivatives as potent anticancer agents: SAR insights and biological profiling
task: Design and evaluation of pyrimidine-based sulfonamide anticancer agents
key_results: Compounds 18, 21, 23, 24 show IC50 7.4–10.2 µM vs HepG2 and 8.2–10.0 µM vs MCF-7, approaching 5-fluorouracil potency; compound 23 docking score −5.56 kcal/mol vs thymidylate synthase
comments: Solid medicinal chemistry work with SAR insights, though in vitro IC50 values alone do not guarantee clinical efficacy or selectivity.

outputs: Binding affinity scores (kcal/mol); H-bond interaction maps

inputs: Synthetic compounds; HepG2, MCF-7, WI-38, VERO cell lines

hypotheses.hypothesis: Electron-donating groups and balanced lipophilicity optimize cytotoxicity and selectivity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by SAR analysis and docking; compounds 18, 21, 23, 24 identified as leads

title: A length-biased Sujit distribution framework: properties, simulation-based inference, and application to clinical remission data
task: Length-biased Sujit distribution for modeling clinical remission survival data
key_results: Length-biased Sujit (LBSJT) distribution derived and characterized; strong parameter estimation accuracy in Monte Carlo simulations, particularly with increasing sample size; superior fit vs established distributions on oncology remission data
comments: Theoretical statistical contribution with established properties; practical applicability demonstrated but limited to this specific oncology context.

outputs: Distribution parameters; reliability metrics (Bonferroni, Lorenz curves)

model_io.model: Maximum likelihood estimation + Monte Carlo simulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Length-biased Sujit distribution fits clinical remission data better than standard distributions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; LBSJT superior goodness-of-fit in clinical dataset

title: ForamJ – A tool for the reproducible, semi-automated analysis of foraminifera micro computed tomography datasets
task: Semi-automated analysis of foraminiferal micro-CT morphology
key_results: ForamJ ImageJ plugin achieves 0.9981 segmentation accuracy (±0.001) and 0.97 Dice score (±0.008) vs manual annotation with 1.30% difference in calcite volume; 154 seconds median processing per sample
comments: Significant advance in reproducibility and throughput for paleontological imaging; validation against commercial software strengthens adoption potential.

outputs: Test segmentation, chamber isolation, morphological metrics (calcite volume, chamber volumes, surface area, wall thickness, centroids)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semi-automated segmentation and morphological quantification match manual commercial software with high fidelity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Dice score 0.97 ± 0.008, calcite volume difference 1.30% vs Dragonfly

title: Lightweight and interpretable edge intelligence AI with intrusion detection for trustworthy cardiac arrhythmia in medical IoT
task: Lightweight, interpretable arrhythmia detection with security-aware trust assessment for cardiac IoT
key_results: CLARITY-AI 2.0 achieves F1 = 0.928, AUC = 0.985 (MIT-BIH); generalizes on PTB-XL (AUC 0.940) and Chapman (AUC 0.915); ESP32 deployment footprint 350 KB, latency 8.1 ms
comments: Excellent balance of clinical performance, interpretability, and edge feasibility; intrusion detection and SHAP explanations strengthen clinical trustworthiness.

outputs: Arrhythmia classification (F1, AUC); trust score; attack probability

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intrusion detection + trust scoring improve inference reliability under adversarial network conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; flagging unreliable inputs; zero-shot generalization on unseen datasets (AUC 0.915–0.940)

title: Wearable sensor big data analysis reveals spatiotemporal injury patterns in professional tennis players
task: Spatiotemporal injury patterns in professional tennis via wearable sensor big data
key_results: Peak injury frequencies during intensive training phases; shoulder-elbow complex 47.3%, lumbar-hip 31.8%; 73.2% injury variance explained by spatiotemporal coupling; Transformer prediction model 91.5% accuracy, AUC 0.956
comments: Comprehensive big data framework for injury epidemiology; high predictive accuracy suggests practical potential for injury prevention systems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatiotemporal coupling of injury distributions and temporal patterns explains injury variance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 73.2% of variance explained by interaction

title: A species rules syntax model accurately organizes birdsong syllables into songs
task: Predicting birdsong syntax from acoustic-sequence rules
key_results: Species rules models predicted syllable sequence accuracy matching tutor songs in both estrildid finch species across colonies; consistent species-specific syntax learned from syllable acoustics and sequence position
comments: Elegant application of NLP-inspired methods to animal behavior; demonstrates that learned syntax follows systematic species-specific rules rather than individual tutoring.

inputs: Syllable acoustic features; sequence position; conspecific song recordings
outputs: Predicted syllable sequences in adult birds

hypotheses.hypothesis: Species rules based on syllable acoustics and sequence position predict syntax as well as tutor songs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in both species; species rules and tutor songs achieved equivalent prediction accuracy

title: Novel explainable deep learning based drug sensitivity prediction for early treatment of breast cancer
task: Explainable drug sensitivity prediction for breast cancer
key_results: Explainable Drug Graph Attention Transformer (EDrGAT) achieves R² = 0.93, outperforming state-of-the-art models; integrates feature-level graph attention with temporal pharmacodynamics features
comments: Strong performance on drug sensitivity prediction, but lack of validation on independent test cohorts and clinical applicability remain unclear.

inputs: Cell line identity, drug name, drug concentration; temporal pharmacodynamics (lag, rolling mean, exponential mean)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Task-specific graph attention and temporal features improve drug sensitivity prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; EDrGAT achieves R² = 0.93 vs prior state-of-the-art

title: In silico evaluation of FDA-approved antivirals and corticosteroids against SARS-CoV-2
task: In silico evaluation of FDA-approved antivirals against SARS-CoV-2
key_results: Baloxavir Marboxil (BM) highest docking affinity (−8.88 ± 0.57 kcal/mol); Dexamethasone (DM) most stable in MD simulations (RMSD 0.78 ± 0.12 Å); all three drugs show RMSD <3.0 Å confirming complex stability
comments: Computational screening identifies candidates but ADMET concerns (BM/RD hepatotoxicity) and lack of experimental validation limit clinical translation.

inputs: Baloxavir Marboxil, Dexamethasone, Remdesivir; SARS-CoV-2 proteins

hypotheses.hypothesis: BM exhibits highest binding affinity; DM most stable; all show suitable pharmacological profiles
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; binding affinities confirmed but ADMET concerns identified for BM/RD

title: Uncovering potentially targetable genes in liver fibrosis via bioinformatics and experimental validation
task: Identify druggable genes in liver fibrosis via integrated multi-omics and validation
key_results: Eight upregulated genes (AQP1, CCL19, CXCL6, CXCL9, CXCL10, EPCAM, IGJ, LUM) with good diagnostic performance in liver fibrosis; enriched in immune regulation, ECM remodeling, and inflammatory pathways; AQP1 confirmed by IHC/IF
comments: Systematic integration of public datasets and clinical validation identifies therapeutic candidates, but functional validation of druggability remains preliminary.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intersection of DEGs, WGCNA modules, and druggable genes identifies therapeutically relevant targets in liver fibrosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; eight genes identified with diagnostic relevance

title: Machine learning approach for adult age estimation using dental characteristics on panoramic radiographs
task: Machine learning for adult age estimation from dental characteristics on radiographs
key_results: Extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) best ML model: RMSE 10.88 years, R² = 0.70 vs linear regression (RMSE 12.04, R² = 0.63) on 2,415 individuals aged 20–89; sound/missing teeth most influential predictors
comments: Methodological improvement in dental age estimation, but prediction error magnitude (±11 years) limits clinical utility as standalone tool.

model_io.model: Machine learning ensemble (random forest, XGBoost, k-NN, SVM, decision tree, MLP)
inputs: Encoded dental characteristics from panoramic radiographs; sex

hypotheses.hypothesis: ML methods with one-hot encoding preserve dual-feature information better than linear regression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; XGBoost RMSE 10.88 vs linear regression 12.04 years

title: Temporal Learning with Dynamic Range (TLDR) for modeling recurrent exposure and treatment outcomes
task: Temporal machine learning for predicting post-acute sequelae of COVID-19 (PASC)
key_results: TLDR framework achieves AUROC 0.791 vs 0.668 for atemporal baseline (18.4% improvement); PRAUC 0.590 vs 0.421 (40.14% improvement); lower overfitting index (−0.028) on 85,000+ patient EHR data
comments: Demonstrates clear value of temporal sequencing in clinical prediction; generalizability and robustness advantages are noteworthy.

inputs: Time-stamped EHR features; longitudinal patient records
outputs: PASC risk prediction; AUROC, PRAUC scores

hypotheses.hypothesis: Temporal sequencing and dynamic windows improve PASC risk prediction vs atemporal approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TLDR AUROC 0.791 vs benchmark 0.668

title: Optimising pandemic response through vaccination strategies using neural networks
task: Optimize pandemic vaccination strategies using neural networks and optimal control
key_results: Physics-informed neural networks + deep RL vaccination strategy reduces cumulative hospital-days 85.3%, deaths 84.4%, total costs 22.3% vs no vaccination; exceeds actual government rollout by 4–6 percentage points (COVID-19, Victoria)
comments: Demonstrates feasibility of ML for epidemic control optimization, but counterfactual comparison to actual policy limits real-world validation.

inputs: Stochastic SVEI3RD compartmental model (8 state variables); COVID-19 data (Victoria)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimal vaccination strategy derived via neural networks minimizes pandemic expenditures vs actual rollout
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; simulated strategy achieves 22.3% cost reduction, exceeding actual by 4–6%

title: Multi-omics evaluation of cell lines as models for metastatic prostate cancer
task: Multi-omics evaluation of cell line suitability for metastatic prostate cancer modeling
key_results: Substantial genomic differences between cell lines and metastatic samples; PC3 shows poor transcriptomic/epigenomic similarity to all subtypes; mesenchymal stem-like organoids exhibit higher fidelity than engineered cell lines
comments: Important resource characterization study revealing limitations of widely-used models; guides future model selection but doesn't provide actionable therapeutic targets.

inputs: Public multi-omics data from cell lines, patient metastatic samples, organoids
outputs: Similarity matrices, subtype associations, mutation burden correlation with microenvironment

hypotheses.hypothesis: PC3 cell line recapitulates metastatic prostate cancer transcriptomics
hypotheses.result: Rejected; PC3 exhibits poor similarity to all subtypes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hypermutation influences tumor microenvironment composition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mutational burden significantly associated with immune infiltration

title: BODIES: BOdy shape parameter and 3D meshes of Individuals basEd on SUPR
task: Synthetic 3D avatar generation using SUPR parametric model
key_results: 84,000 synthetic subjects; SUPR outperforms prior representations for avatar mesh generation
comments: Synthetic generation avoids privacy issues but validation against real human body diversity is not explicitly addressed.

title: A Topology Standardized 3D Facial Dataset with Emotion and Action Unit Diversity for East Asians
task: 3D facial dataset construction with standardized topology and emotion diversity
key_results: 98 East Asian subjects; 6 emotions + 9 FACS action units; unified mesh topology via FLAME + Edge-NICP
comments: Topology standardization enables direct comparisons but small sample size (N=98) limits generalizability; multi-view RGB only for subset.

title: Computational investigation on the hydrodynamic performance of a vertically submerged plate-type wave energy converter under variable relative openings
task: Wave energy converter hydrodynamic efficiency under variable seabed and wave conditions
key_results: Optimal efficiency at relative opening α=50%; elevated axial velocity at T=1.87s for high steepness; uneven seabed reduces efficiency vs. flat bottom
comments: Numerical tank (VOF/Fluent) well-validated but sensitivity to turbulence model choice not explored; field testing needed for practical WEC design.

model_io.model: ANSYS Fluent (VOF method, Stokes wave generation)

title: Fluid–structure interaction and thermal performance: a numerical study on crossflow heat exchangers with aerodynamically optimised splitter elements
task: Crossflow heat exchanger performance with optimized splitter geometry via fluid-structure interaction
key_results: Backwards-facing splitters enhance Nusselt number (Nu) while maintaining Performance Evaluation Criterion (PEC>1); optimal L/D balances heat gain vs. pressure drop; 3D/2D validation ±12–14% vs. experiment
comments: Rigorous CFD with validation and practical design guidelines; structural stress on splitter plates and real-world fouling not addressed.

title: Neuroimaging-driven recommendation systems for personalized sports training and injury prevention
task: Neuroimaging-driven personalized sports training and injury prevention recommendation
key_results: NeuroAthleteNet + NeuroSportSync multimodal framework improves predictive accuracy and interpretability over traditional neuroimaging analysis
comments: Novel integration of fMRI/EEG with biomechanics is conceptually sound but no quantitative performance metrics provided; reproducibility unclear without code/data release.

title: A multi dataset validation model for hybrid feature selection in wind energy maximum power point tracking systems
task: Wind energy MPPT feature selection via hybrid MI filtering and multi-objective binary harmony search
key_results: Dimensionality reduction 76.9–87.5% (464→58–97 features Kelmarsh; 87→8–21 VV); RMSE improvements 9.4–14.7% over full-feature baseline; 20.6–28.6% vs. MI alone
comments: Comprehensive multi-dataset validation (Kelmarsh, VV, lab) demonstrates robustness; 87.5% reduction critical for real-time SCADA deployment; statistical rigor (p<0.001, CV<8%) strong.

model_io.model: Mutual Information filtering + AMO-BHS optimization + Random Forest regression

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid MI + AMO-BHS outperforms full-feature and single-method FS approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 9.4–14.7% accuracy improvement; 20.6–28.6% vs. MI alone

title: A blockchain-integrated multi-scale convolutional network for accurate recognition and secure tracking of high-difficulty movements in aerobics training
task: Aerobics movement recognition and secure training data management via CNN and blockchain
key_results: 96.8% accuracy on custom aerobics dataset; blockchain integration increases traceability efficiency by 38.2%
comments: Multi-scale convolution + dual attention is effective but blockchain overhead for real-time systems not quantified; generalization beyond aerobics unclear.

model_io.model: Multi-channel multi-scale CNN + dual attention + blockchain

title: Analytical modeling of pcm-based cooling system for lithium-ion batteries
task: Analytical thermal modeling of PCM-cooled lithium-ion batteries
key_results: Perturbation + Green's function solution predicts temperature profiles with <5% error; core temperature remains hotter than surface; improving battery thermal conductivity crucial
comments: Analytical solution avoids computational expense of FEM/CFD but assumes idealized geometry; practical pack design integration not addressed.

title: Reading the climate room through unsupervised analysis of unfiltered climate perspectives
task: Unsupervised discovery of climate discourse rhetorical strategies
key_results: Identified distinctive advocate (pragmatic/crisis) vs. skeptic (anti-elite/anger) rhetorical patterns; 48,443 documents corpus released publicly
comments: Large-scale dataset is valuable, but unsupervised categorization may conflate conceptually distinct framings without external validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Advocates and skeptics employ fundamentally different rhetorical strategies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: advocates emphasize solutions/crisis framing; skeptics use anti-elite rhetoric and anger appeals

title: A bearing fault diagnosis method for complex system based on improved extended belief rule base
task: Bearing fault diagnosis under imbalanced data via ensemble EBRB with KL divergence
key_results: KL divergence-based EBRB-EU outperforms Euclidean distance-based EBRB; ensemble undersampling + differential evolution optimization improves accuracy and noise robustness
comments: Ensemble approach and metric choice (KL divergence) are principled but computational complexity of multi-subset training not discussed.

model_io.model: Ensemble EBRB-EU with KL divergence + differential evolution

hypotheses.hypothesis: KL divergence captures probability distribution differences better than Euclidean distance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: KL-based model outperforms Euclidean baseline

title: A unified time series classification framework via adaptive Gaussian image representation
task: Unified time series classification via adaptive Gaussian image representation
key_results: TS2Vision achieves Rank-1 consistency on 158 UCR/UEA datasets (univariate + multivariate); Lipschitz continuity proven theoretically
comments: Novel ATSGM mapping and theoretical guarantee are strengths; computational cost of circle-packing optimization not explicitly analyzed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive Gaussian image mapping outperforms static encodings for heterogeneous time series
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Rank-1 consistency across 158 datasets significantly exceeds baselines

title: A SMT pin soldering defect detection system based on improved connectivity domain algorithm
task: SMT solder defect detection via improved connected domain + watershed segmentation
key_results: Hybrid algorithm reduces false-positive rate in dense soldering; maintains accuracy of traditional connected-component labeling with better robustness to interference
comments: Engineering-focused improvement; quantitative metrics (false-positive reduction %) not explicitly reported; benchmark dataset not publicly released.

title: A scalable hybrid computational intelligence framework with bio inspired optimization for high dimensional malicious URL inference
task: Malicious URL detection via hybrid discriminant analysis and bio-inspired optimization
key_results: Bio-inspired optimized model (MOA + Osprey) achieves accuracy 96.35%, precision 96.54%, F1=96.40% on 63,191 URLs; SHAP feature attribution provides transparency
comments: Strong accuracy and comprehensive statistical validation (Shapiro–Wilk, Kruskal–Wallis); scalability to real-time detection not addressed.

model_io.model: LDA/QDA + XGBoost + Mother Optimization Algorithm + Osprey Optimization

title: Water treatment chemical supply chain disruption risk assessment and mitigation strategy ranking using BWM–VIKOR
task: Multi-criteria decision framework for water treatment supply chain resilience
key_results: BWM-VIKOR identifies compliance/health (0.26) and service continuity (0.18) as top priorities; dual sourcing + prequalification optimal compromise (Q=0.083)
comments: Structured MCDM approach is transparent but relies on expert weightings that may vary by context; sensitivity analysis validates robustness.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual sourcing and supplier prequalification provide best compromise under baseline conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Q=0.083, S=0.563 best performance

title: Collective and augmented intelligence outperform artificial intelligence on emotion recognition tests
task: Collective vs. individual AI performance on emotion recognition
key_results: GPT-5 mini outperforms individuals on RMET/MRMET but wisdom-of-crowds (plurality voting) outperforms GPT-5 mini; augmented human-AI aggregation best overall
comments: Critical finding that average humans underestimate collective capability; small sample size for individual comparisons not discussed.

model_io.model: GPT-5 mini, human subjects, plurality voting aggregation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Wisdom-of-crowds aggregation outperforms individual AI performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: human plurality voting significantly surpasses GPT-5 mini

hypotheses.hypothesis: Augmented human-AI aggregation achieves superior performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: combined approach outperforms either source alone

title: Role of FePt grain size on writing performance for next-generation magnetic recording technology
task: Magnetic recording grain size optimization for HAMR/HDMR technologies
key_results: 5 nm grains optimal for areal density (16.4 Tb/in²); 3–4 nm grains achieve higher density but with elevated BER; higher field/damping mitigates error
comments: Atomistic simulation provides mechanistic insights but extrapolation to manufacturing-scale variability requires empirical validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Smaller grains improve areal density but increase BER due to thermal fluctuations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 3–4 nm grains show higher AD but elevated BER; 5 nm is optimal trade-off

title: A novel nowcasting (estimation) model based on an adaptive network neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy inference system (ANNHFIS): a case study of Istanbul
task: NO₂ level nowcasting near biomass plants via neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy inference
key_results: ANNHFIS-PSO achieves RMSE=3.6488 µg/m³, R²=0.8938; outperforms MLP-ANN, ANFIS variants, LSTM on Istanbul biomass plant data
comments: Novel uncertainty representation via neutrosophic fuzzy sets is innovative but interpretability of membership functions not discussed; generalization to other pollutants unclear.

model_io.model: ANNHFIS-PSO (adaptive neuro-fuzzy + neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy + particle swarm optimization)

title: Euler simulation of hydrocyclone pre-separation before oily wastewater membrane treatment based on Population balance model
task: Hydrocyclone pre-separation of oily wastewater via population balance modeling
key_results: Oil volume fraction distribution controlled by inlet velocity, oil content, cone angle, overflow depth; high flow (20 m/s) causes droplet breakup; optimal efficiency at 5–15 m/s
comments: Detailed CFD analysis is thorough but population balance model parameters (coalescence, breakup kernels) not explicitly discussed; pilot-scale validation missing.

model_io.model: Euler finite volume method + population balance model

title: In-sensor cryptographic signature generation to link a physical process and an immutable digital entity
task: Cryptographic sensor for linking physical recording to immutable digital blockchain entry
key_results: Proof-of-concept in 180 nm CMOS; monolithic sensor with integrated hashing and public-key signing prevents data forgery via blockchain link
comments: Novel anti-tampering approach addresses societal concern about deepfakes/AI manipulation; scalability to high-resolution video and practical deployment barriers not discussed.

title: A Monolithic CMOS-MEMS SoC with 1.8 mm/s and 2 mK Resolution for Flow and Temperature Sensing via a Microcantilever Array
task: Demonstrating high-sensitivity CMOS-MEMS multiparameter sensing via microcantilever array
key_results: Temperature sensitivity 25.1 kHz/°C (linearity <0.3%), resolution 2.3 mK; flow velocity sensitivity 32.76 kHz/(m/s) at 130 m/s, resolution 1.8 mm/s; Allan deviation 14.8 Hz
comments: Monolithic 0.18 µm CMOS integration with post-processing achieves ultrahigh sensitivity; compact footprint (SoC) suitable for miniaturized environmental/biomedical applications.

model_io.model: Capacitive microcantilever array with CMOS signal processing

hypotheses.hypothesis: Monolithic CMOS-MEMS achieves 2.3 mK temperature resolution
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; measured resolution matches specification

title: Tunable and nonlinearity-enhanced dispersive-plus-dissipative coupling in photon-pressure circuits
task: Realizing tunable photon-pressure circuits with nonlinearity-enhanced coupling
key_results: Magnetic-flux-tunable dispersive-dissipative coupling; multi-photon rates scale faster than √nc; Fano-like transparency and parametric instability observed
comments: Novel exploitation of superconducting circuit nonlinearities to exceed standard optomechanical scaling; demonstrates rich dynamical phenomena beyond conventional optomechanics.

model_io.model: Cavity optomechanical Hamiltonian in superconducting circuits

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonlinearities enhance photon-pressure coupling beyond √nc scaling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; multi-photon coupling rates demonstrated with stronger pump dependence

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interference of coupling paths produces Fano-like response
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; photon-pressure induced transparency shows Fano line shape

title: Emergent discrete space-time crystal of Majorana-like quasiparticles in chiral liquid crystals
task: Observing discrete space-time crystals in driven chiral liquid crystals
key_results: Classical 1+1D and 2+1D discrete space-time crystals observed; period-doubling from topological Majorana-like features; robust to perturbations and defects
comments: First classical realization of space-time crystals combining topological and temporal symmetry breaking; extends time crystal concept beyond quantum systems.

model_io.model: Chiral liquid crystal driven by Floquet electrical signal

hypotheses.hypothesis: Period-doubling arises from topological Majorana-like quasiparticle features
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; inter-transformations of solitons/disclinations enable symmetry breaking

title: Modulation of quantum geometry and its coupling to pseudo-electric field by dynamic strain
task: Dynamically modulating quantum geometry and Berry curvature in 2D materials
key_results: Berry curvature and dipole moments modulated in real-time via oscillatory strain; pseudo-electric field coupling generates unusual dynamic Hall response; frequency mixing observed
comments: Novel approach to quantum geometry control beyond static perturbations; demonstrates external field-free anomalous Hall response via Berry curvature engineering.

model_io.model: 2D heterostructure on membrane under combined strain and AC field

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pseudo-electric field couples to Berry curvature under dynamic strain
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; unusual strain-induced Hall response observed at combination frequencies

title: Linking photoelectron circular dichroism to the asymmetric total photoemission yield measured in aerosol nanoparticles of tyrosine
task: Detecting molecular chirality via photoelectron circular dichroism in aerosol particles
key_results: Chiral asymmetry of photoemission yield (CAPY) detected without vacuum/spectrometer; electric dipole origin; high sensitivity to enantiopurity
comments: Translates PECD phenomenon to practical analytical tool for particulate matter; enables chirality detection in environmental/biomedical samples without specialized apparatus.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PECD in angular distribution translates to asymmetry in total photoemission yield
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CAPY mediated by light attenuation in particles

title: Crystal symmetry-dependent Orbital Rashba Edelstein effect in epitaxial CuO thin film
task: Revealing crystal symmetry effects on orbital Rashba-Edelstein effect in CuO
key_results: Four-fold anisotropy in orbital Rashba-Edelstein effect; sign change of spin torque efficiency; perpendicular magnetization switching demonstrated
comments: First-principles calculations and symmetry-based analysis establish crystal structure as determinant of orbital angular momentum anisotropy; promising for orbitronic device design.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Crystal symmetry governs anisotropy of orbital Rashba-Edelstein effect
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; four-fold anisotropy observed matching crystal symmetry

title: Symmetry-broken Kondo screening and zero-energy mode in a kagome superconductor
task: Observing symmetry-broken Kondo screening in kagome superconductor CsV3Sb5
key_results: Kondo resonance near Cr impurities breaks in-plane mirror symmetry; zero-bias peak from V vacancies; evidence for orbital loop current order
comments: Reveals interplay of impurity screening and underlying electronic chirality; suggests kagome superconductor hosts unconventional order.

model_io.model: CsV3Sb5 with magnetic dopants and vacancies

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magnetic impurities trigger Kondo screening with broken mirror symmetry
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Kondo screening pattern breaks all in-plane mirror symmetries

title: Compact multi-band bandpass filter with independently controlled passbands using CRLH-TZRP
task: Designing multi-band bandpass filter with independently controlled passbands
key_results: CRLH-TZRP concept yields four transmission zeros and three poles; two-filter configuration achieves compact multiband design (10.4×10.8×0.64 mm³); good out-of-band rejection
comments: Microstrip implementation demonstrates practical feasibility for Wi-Fi/WiMAX/5G; transmission zero allocation strategy enables independent passband tuning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CRLH-TZRP allocates transmission zeros to enable multiband passband design
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; filter prototype shows good agreement with simulation

title: Chaotic fluctuations mark the sign of mental activity in task-based heart rate variability
task: Detecting mental activity via nonlinear analysis of heart rate variability
key_results: Chaos/complexity indices increase significantly during mental tasks; conventional linear indices unchanged; nonlinear measures uniquely sensitive to cognition
comments: Novel application of chaos theory to brain-heart interactions; demonstrates superior sensitivity of nonlinear HRV analysis for cognitive state detection.

model_io.model: Autonomic nervous system regulation of heart rate

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chaotic features emerge in HRV during cognitive activity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; chaos/complexity indices elevated during mental tasks

title: Radiation-resistant C-type dipole dump magnet for MIR/THz FEL applications at TARLA
task: Designing radiation-resistant dipole dump magnet for MIR/THz FEL
key_results: C-type magnet deflects 45 MeV beams by 42°; coil temperature rise only 18.7°C; neutron shielding achieves 99% attenuation; radiation-tolerant design validated
comments: Combined MCNP-COMSOL analysis demonstrates effective radiation mitigation; electrical steel selection and parallel winding optimize thermal margins.

model_io.model: C-type dipole dump magnet for TARLA FEL

hypotheses.hypothesis: Radiation and resistive heating cause coil temperature rise <20°C
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 18.7°C rise allows air-cooled operation

title: 2D materials assisted terahertz modulators and sensors
task: Reviewing 2D material-based terahertz modulators and sensors
key_results: Graphene, TMDs, MXenes enable subwavelength THz manipulation; atomic-scale thickness allows phase/amplitude/polarization control; key mechanisms identified
comments: Comprehensive survey of 2D materials for THz applications; identifies graphene, TMDs, MXenes as leading platforms with advantages in bandwidth and integration.

title: Progress and prospects in the underground laboratories' search for dark matter
task: Reviewing dark matter detection progress in underground laboratories
key_results: Multiple experimental technologies developed for diverse dark matter particle searches; improved sensitivities across parameter space; cross-validation capabilities
comments: Comprehensive perspective on underground dark matter experiments; prospects for future detectors improving sensitivity remain open.

title: Collective dynamics of polar nanoregions enhance electrical properties via solid-solution-induced entropy increase in KBT relaxors
task: Enhancing relaxor ferroelectric properties via polar nanoregion collective dynamics
key_results: 10-1000 nm PNR mesostructures self-assemble from 2-4 nm PNRs via Turing instability; jamming effects enhance electrostrain and energy storage; entropy-doping strategy effective
comments: Identifies self-organization mechanism (Turing instability) underlying PNR collective behavior; establishes structure-property relationships for high-performance relaxors.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PNR mesostructures form via Turing instability in nanoregion groups
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Gray-Scott model predicts observed 10-1000 nm structures

title: Mean field magnetism and spin frustration in a double perovskite oxide with compositional complexity
task: Understanding magnetic ordering in high-entropy double perovskite oxide films
key_results: Curie temperature ~150 K despite compositional disorder; reentrant spin-glass behavior at low temperature; mean-field approach captures long-range ordering
comments: Demonstrates robustness of ferromagnetism to high cationic disorder; microscopic interactions become relevant below Tc, indicating complexity beyond mean-field at low T.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mean-field approach predicts Curie temperature from average RE ionic radius
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Tc ≈150 K consistent with prediction

hypotheses.hypothesis: Low-temperature magnetic behavior deviates from mean-field due to RE ion contributions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reentrant spin-glass-like behavior observed

title: Signed, sealed, delivered: a generalizable model for living biotherapeutic dosing and metabolism
task: Predict living biotherapeutic dosing and efficacy for gut microbiome-mediated metabolic disorders
key_results: BCAT model achieves 5% mean prediction error versus ~30% for prior models; predicts ~10^9 CFU probiotic dose 3–4 h before meals achieves 95% reduction in systemic trimethylamine in TMAU
comments: Strong mechanistic framework coupling transit and metabolism, though validation limited to two specific clinical trials; enzyme expression identified as dominant design parameter suggests potential generalizability.

model_io.model: Bacterial Compartment Absorption and Transit (BCAT) pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic model

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mechanistically-derived compartmental model with dosing time optimization generalizes across gut microbiome-mediated disorders
hypotheses.result: Confirmed for choline metabolism and SYNB1618 trials; predicted efficacy for TMAU supported by sensitivity analysis
